Rationale is described in the context of existing knowledge about migraine and ketorolac.
Objectives are explicitly stated to evaluate the efficacy of ketorolac in treating acute migraine.
Inclusion and exclusion criteria are specified, including study types and patient characteristics.
All databases and sources searched are specified, along with the date of the last search.
Full search strategies for databases are presented, including filters and limits used.
Methods for study selection are specified, including independent review by two authors.
Data collection methods are specified, including independent data extraction by reviewers.
Outcomes for data collection are listed and defined, including primary and secondary outcomes.
Other variables for data collection are listed, and assumptions about missing data are described.
Methods for risk of bias assessment are specified, including tools and independent review.
Effect measures for outcomes are specified, including risk ratios and standardized mean differences.
Processes for deciding study eligibility for synthesis are described, including comparisons made.
Methods for handling missing data and data conversions are described.
Methods for tabulating and visually displaying results are described, including tables and figures.
Methods for synthesizing results are described, including meta-analysis models and software used.
Methods for exploring heterogeneity are described, including subgroup analysis.
Sensitivity analyses conducted to assess robustness of results are described.
Methods for assessing risk of bias due to missing results are described.
Methods for assessing certainty in the body of evidence are described using GRADE methodology.
Results of the search and selection process are described, including a flow diagram.
Excluded studies are cited with reasons for exclusion provided.
Each included study is cited, and its characteristics are presented in a table.
Risk of bias assessments for each included study are presented.
Summary statistics and effect estimates for each study are presented in tables.
Characteristics and risk of bias among contributing studies are summarized for each synthesis.
Results of all statistical syntheses, including meta-analyses, are presented with summary estimates.
Results of investigations into heterogeneity among study results are presented.
Results of sensitivity analyses conducted to assess robustness are presented.
Assessments of risk of bias due to missing results are presented for each synthesis.
Certainty assessments for each outcome are presented using GRADE methodology.
General interpretation of results is provided in the context of other evidence.
Limitations of the evidence included in the review are discussed.
Limitations of the review processes used are discussed.
Implications of the results for practice, policy, and future research are discussed.
Registration information for the review is provided, including the register name and number.
Location of the review protocol is indicated, with registration in PROSPERO.
No amendments to information provided at registration or in the protocol are described.
Sources of financial or non-financial support for the review are described.
Competing interests of review authors are not declared.
Availability of data, code, and other materials is not reported.
Title identifies this as a systematic review and meta-analysis.
The abstract provides an explicit statement of the main objective.
Inclusion criteria are specified as RCTs comparing ketorolac to other medications for migraine.
